message:
fix: handle NULL agent_id in find_context_source_conversation return

Follow-on to the recent batch of NULL-agent legacy-V1 fixes (ac1a62ae,
a48e6394, 34ac636e, 6cdb077d, 59cd3696).

Problem:
- find_context_source_conversation() returned (i64, i64, ...), with the
  second field typed as a non-null agent_id.  Legacy V1 conversation
  rows carry agent_id = NULL, so the old signature was silently unsound
  for that slice of the corpus: frankensqlite would either error on
  row decode or — worse — return a mis-materialized 0 that happens to
  match a real agent.
- Downstream, run_context()'s "same agent" related-session lookup
  blindly used that agent_id, so it would return unrelated rows for
  legacy conversations.

Fix:
- Change the signature's second tuple slot to Option<i64> so the NULL
  state is modeled explicitly, and add a doc comment spelling out the
  contract.
- In run_context(), wrap the same_agent query in
  `if let Some(agent_id) = agent_id { ... } else { Vec::new() }` so
  legacy NULL-agent source rows skip the "same agent" section entirely
  instead of issuing a bogus query.

Cargo.lock:
- Transitive refresh: fsqlite-wal (or sibling in the workspace) now
  pulls in `dashmap` as a new dependency edge.  No direct dependency
  bump on our side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
